title: Cannot compile intentionally empty modules anymore in Yosys 0.9
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

I have the following code in a file called test.v:

```
module top (hz100, reset, pb, ss7, ss6, ss5, ss4, ss3, ss2, ss1, ss0, left, right, red, green, blue);
  input hz100, reset;
  input [20:0] pb;
  output [7:0] ss7, ss6, ss5, ss4, ss3, ss2, ss1, ss0, left, right;
  output red, green, blue;
  
endmodule 
```

which is intended to be empty, since I use Yosys in another software which verifies if the code is syntactically correct. I attempted to compile this code with the command:

`yosys -p "synth_ice40 -top top -blif test.blif" test.v`

And the version of Yosys I'm using is Yosys 0.9+932 (git sha1 3c6a566d, gcc 6.3.0-18+deb9u1 -fPIC -Os).

## Actual behavior

yosys ends at the following step:

```
...
2.47. Printing statistics.

2.48. Executing CHECK pass (checking for obvious problems).
found and reported 0 problems.

2.49. Executing BLIF backend.
ERROR: Can't find top module `\top'!
```

## Expected behavior

This error did not happen in Yosys 0.8. It correctly compiled and produced a test.blif file as expected.
code_blocks:
...
2.47. Printing statistics.

2.48. Executing CHECK pass (checking for obvious problems).
found and reported 0 problems.

2.49. Executing BLIF backend.
ERROR: Can't find top module `\top'!
